mission.title: Install and Configure Velero (Backup & Restore Operator)
mission.description: Velero is a backup and restore operator for Kubernetes that allows you to back up your cluster resources and persistent volumes, and restore them in case of loss. It is useful for migrating cluster resources between environments and replicating production clusters for testing. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration of cloud storage for backups and potential complexity in restoring from backups.

steps.title: Add the Velero Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Velero Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vmware-tanzu https://vmware-tanzu.github.io/helm-charts

```

> This is the official repository for Velero.

steps.title: Install Velero CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Velero to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/releases/download/v1.18.0/velero-crds.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains the necessary CRDs for Velero.

steps.title: Install Velero using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Velero in the specified namespace with the necessary configuration.

```bash

helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=<YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=<YOUR_AWS_REGION> --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=<YOUR_AWS_REGION>

```

> Replace <YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> and <YOUR_AWS_REGION> with your actual AWS S3 bucket name and region.
commands: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=<YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=<YOUR_AWS_REGION> --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=<YOUR_AWS_REGION>

steps.title: Verify Velero installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Velero server is running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> You should see the Velero pod in a Running state.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Velero
uninstall.description:
This step removes Velero and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all backups stored in the specified bucket, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall velero --namespace velero

kubectl delete namespace velero

```

> This command removes the Velero release.
> This command deletes the Velero namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Velero
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current Velero configuration and data. This step will upgrade Velero to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl get backups.velero.io --all-namespaces

helm upgrade velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --version 1.18.0

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> List all backups to ensure you have a backup before proceeding.
> This command upgrades Velero to the specified version.
> Verify that the Velero pod is running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create backup: no valid backup storage location found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the backup storage location is not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Check your Helm install command to ensure the bucket name and region are correctly set.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Velero server is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Velero pod may not have started due to configuration issues or insufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Velero pod using 'kubectl logs <velero-pod-name> -n velero' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Backup failed: unable to access the backup storage location'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that Velero cannot access the specified S3 bucket.

**Fix:**
Verify your AWS credentials and permissions for the S3 bucket.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Volume snapshot location not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error occurs when the specified volume snapshot location is not configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the volume snapshot location is correctly defined in your Helm install command.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.35, improved AWS S3 integration, and enhanced error messages.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy volume snapshot locations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.17 or earlier, ensure to update your volume snapshot locations to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the Velero pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n velero'.
codeSnippets: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=<YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.config.region=<YOUR_AWS_REGION> --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.config.region=<YOUR_AWS_REGION>

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
